extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož attacked solo on the final steep climb into Trevi, held off YATES Adam's late surge by 3 seconds to win stage 3
tactical_patterns: Roglič's early solo move on steep finale succeeded despite elite chase group; late counters failed against sustained power effort.
Mitchelton-Scott hit twice by crashes at 30km and earlier, disrupting their protective formation for race situations.
Van Avermaet initiated GC group move on climb but got immediately countered by Moscon, revealing BMC lacked chase coordination.
Thomas sat on Moscon's wheel conservatively, let Yates counter, settled for 4th but won GC on countback over Van Avermaet.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: sustained solo power on steep finale defied conventional wisdom that early moves fail against stacked GC field
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: nearly closed significant gap with explosive late counter, confirming sharp climbing form despite conservative team tactics
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: tactical conservatism on wheel got 4th place but inherited GC lead on countback, questionable attacking intent
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: negative: caught in Geschke/Bora crash, lost all GC contention on decisive climb, poor positioning cost entire race
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: third place finish after Strade Bianche win confirms early-season form peak across different terrain types
rider_signals.BEVIN Patrick: negative: race leader crashed out of contention well before finale, lost jersey in non-racing incident
